main.description: A hypercert record tracking impact work.

shortDescription.description: Short blurb of the impact work done.

description.description: Optional longer description of the impact work done.

image.description: The hypercert visual representation as a URI or image blob.

workScope.description: A strong reference to a record defining the scope of work. The record referenced should describe the logical scope using label-based conditions.

contributors.description: An array of contributor objects, each containing contributor information, weight, and contribution details.

rights.description: A strong reference to the rights that this hypercert has. The record referenced must conform with the lexicon org.hypercerts.claim.rights.

locations.description: An array of strong references to the location where activity was performed. The record referenced must conform with the lexicon app.certified.location.

createdAt.description: Client-declared timestamp when this record was originally created

contributorInformation.description: Contributor information as a string (DID or identifier) or strong reference to for instance org.hypercerts.claim.contributorInformation#main.

weight.description: The relative weight/importance of this contribution (stored as a string to avoid float precision issues). Must be a positive numeric value. Weights do not need to sum to a specific total; normalization can be performed by the consuming application as needed.

contributionDetails.description: Contribution details as a string or strong reference to org.hypercerts.claim.contributionDetails#main.

contributorIdentity.description: Contributor information as a string (DID or identifier).

activity.description: A strong reference to a hypercert activity record. This activity must conform to the lexicon org.hypercerts.claim.activity

weight.description: The relative weight/importance of this hypercert activity (stored as a string to avoid float precision issues). Weights can be any positive numeric values and do not need to sum to a specific total; normalization can be performed by the consuming application as needed.
